title: nicotiana rosulata
intro: Nicotiana rosulata, also known as wild tobacco or Aztec tobacco, is a plant species belonging to the nightshade family, characterized by its fragrant, white flowers and its ability to produce nicotine, a stimulant. Traditionally, Nicotiana rosulata has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a treatment for respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Nicotiana rosulata include its ability to soothe and calm the respiratory system, reduce stress and anxiety, and even act as an antispasmodic to alleviate muscle cramps. Herbal preparations of Nicotiana rosulata can be made by drying the leaves and flowers, and then infusing them in hot water to make a tea, or by using them in tinctures and salves for topical application.
